summary: Apparatus-leak: editorial front-matter chunked as primary text across multiple sources (Mead/Waite/Bellows/Charles/Taylor headers, notes, untranslated-Greek fragments), polluting retrieval and drawing spurious staged_edges

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Surfaced during the corpus-wide 0.85 PARALLELS edge review (guru-review-edges). A recurring family of chunks are editorial apparatus, not primary text, and they draw spurious edges — several with hallucinated justifications (the model invents a shared theme present in neither chunk).

Sources observed leaking apparatus into retrievable chunks:
- corpus-hermeticum-*: G.R.S. Mead translator headers/intros (e.g. 'The Corpus Hermeticum translated by G.R.S. Mead. I. Poemandres... <This is the most famous...>') returned instead of the sermon body. (edges ~18716/18723/18725/18757/18762/18793)
- paracelsus-aurora / paracelsus-tincture: A.E. Waite editorial notes — Aurora authenticity discussion ('genuine work of the Sage of Hohenheim, yet in some respects [spurious]') and pseudo-Hermetic-authorship note ('Among the many writings fabulously attributed to Hermes...'). (edges ~18753-18757, 18792-18794)
- iamblichus-on-the-mysteries: Thomas Taylor untranslated-Greek footnote fragments (the 'και αετοι μεν, και δρακοντες...' animal-symbols note) and bibliographic citation notes (his Plato translation, Proclus-on-Cratylus scholia). (edges ~18418-18420, 18724)
- poetic-edda-voluspo: Bellows editorial section-notes (Loki/Lothur, Odin's knowledge-quest) drawing spurious Egyptian<->Norse edges. (edges ~18433-18435)
- enoch-charles-1917: R.H. Charles section-summaries (scholar's voice, e.g. 'The Apocalyptist prophesies further of the repentance of the Gentiles...').
- orphic-hymns: Thomas Taylor prefatory 'Dissertation on the Life and Theology of Orpheus' front-matter.

Separate likely-bug: corpus-hermeticum-15 and corpus-hermeticum-16 returned IDENTICAL body text ('For there are many choirs of daimons round Him...') — possible duplicate chunk across two text_ids; verify and dedup.

Fix direction (pipeline-side, not hand-edits): extend scripts/chunk.py is_apparatus_chunk() / per-config drop rules (drop_chunk_patterns, pre_strip_patterns) to catch (a) translator/editor header blocks ('translated by', bracketed editorial notes, section-title+intro), (b) editor's-voice summaries, (c) untranslated-Greek-only fragments, (d) bibliographic citation notes. Same class as the Yasna/Plotinus/Zhuangzi apparatus cleanup (todo:2957d758); after fixing, re-chunk + body-matched remap (scripts/migrations/apparatus_remap.py) to preserve curation.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: DUPLICATION confirmed (acquisition-level, distinct from apparatus): corpus-hermeticum-15 and corpus-hermeticum-16 share 5 of 6 byte-identical chunk bodies. Both contain libellus XVI ('CORPUS HERMETICUM (XVI.) THE DEFINITIONS OF ASCLEPIUS UNTO KING AMMON'), yet their configs/metadata are mislabeled — chunking/corpus-hermeticum-15 says 'CH 15: The Hymn of Hermes' and -16 says 'CH 16: The Secret Sermon on the Mountain'. So the true CH-15 (Hymn) and CH-XIII (Secret Sermon) texts are missing/replaced by a duplicate XVI. Root cause is upstream in acquire.py / the manifest (wrong source URL or duplicate scrape), NOT chunk.py.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Apparatus-leak mechanism confirmed by direct test. The two gates that drop apparatus — BASELINE_PRE_STRIP (chunk.py:72) and is_apparatus_chunk (chunk.py:84) — were tuned for sacred-texts.com boilerplate and both return FALSE on the leaking chunks:
- BASELINE_PRE_STRIP nav regex is anchored to 'Sacred Texts <Collection> Index Previous Next ...'. Mead's edition emits 'Index Previous Next Thrice-Greatest Hermes - Volume 2 by G.R.S. Mead p. 266 ...' (no 'Sacred Texts' prefix) -> no match (CH-16/001). The standalone translator credit 'The Corpus Hermeticum translated by G.R.S. Mead I. Poemandres ... <This is the most famous ...>' also -> no match (CH-01/001).
- is_apparatus_chunk only drops (a) Errata blocks and (b) short 'Next:/Previous:' footers (<=200 chars). The Mead headers are neither -> kept as primary chunks.
- paracelsus-aurora chunk 020.toml IS the Waite authenticity commentary ('genuine work of the Sage of Hohenheim, yet in some respects...'); the config's pre_strip_patterns only strip the leading nav/byline header, so interspersed editor's-voice commentary paragraphs survive as body.
These sources are non-sacred-texts editions (Mead/Hermes, Waite/Paracelsus, Taylor/Iamblichus+Orpheus, Bellows/Edda, Charles/Enoch) whose apparatus forms differ from the boilerplate the gates recognise.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Two independent root causes, not one: (A) CHUNKING apparatus scope-gap — is_apparatus_chunk + BASELINE_PRE_STRIP recognise only sacred-texts.com apparatus, so non-sacred-texts editorial forms (translator-credit headers, 'Index Previous Next <Volume> by <ed>' nav, editorial bracket-notes <...>, editor's-voice section-summaries, interspersed commentary paragraphs, untranslated-Greek-only fragments) pass both gates. (B) ACQUISITION duplication — CH-15/-16 both scraped libellus XVI with mislabeled metadata.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Confirmed two distinct fixes:
(A) Chunking (scripts/chunk.py): broaden the apparatus gates beyond sacred-texts boilerplate. Add to BASELINE_PRE_STRIP a non-prefixed 'Index Previous Next <...> by <editor> p. N' nav strip and a '<Work> translated by <Translator>' credit strip; extend is_apparatus_chunk to drop whole chunks that are (i) a translator/editor header + bracketed <...> intro with little/no primary body, (ii) editor's-voice summary ('The Apocalyptist prophesies...'), (iii) untranslated-Greek-only fragments. Preserve the keep-substantive-body rule (feedback_tag_reject_preface_chunks): only drop when the body really is apparatus, length-independent. Validate 0-false-positive corpus-wide like prior apparatus work. Then re-chunk affected sources and run scripts/migrations/apparatus_remap.py (body-matched) to preserve curation — same pattern as todo:2957d758 (Yasna/Plotinus/Zhuangzi).
(B) Acquisition (acquire.py / manifest): re-acquire corpus-hermeticum-15 (true Hymn of Hermes) and the mislabeled -16 so both hold their correct libellus; verify no other CH source-ids collide. This is independent of (A) and could be split into a child ticket.
Affected sources to re-chunk after (A): all 17 corpus-hermeticum-*, paracelsus-aurora-of-philosophers, paracelsus-tincture-of-philosophers, iamblichus-on-the-mysteries, poetic-edda-voluspo, enoch-charles-1917, orphic-hymns.
